entries.title: Oi prefix on all public symbols
entries.description: Every public widget, class, enum, and extension uses the Oi prefix. Classes are named by what they are in the UI (OiTable, OiButton, OiSidebar).

entries.title: Props read like English
entries.description: Props are named as readable English phrases (multiSelect: true, reorderColumns: true, stickyHeader: true) rather than technical abbreviations or opaque identifiers.

entries.title: Required props enforce correctness
entries.description: Images require alt, buttons (all variants including .icon()) require label, icons require label. These are enforced as Dart required named parameters.

entries.title: Factory constructors for variants
entries.description: OiButton provides factory constructors for each variant: primary(), secondary(), outline(), ghost(), destructive(), soft(), icon(), split(), countdown(), confirm().

entries.title: Callbacks are verbs
entries.description: All callback parameters use the on + verb pattern (onTap, onSave, onSort, onReorder, onChanged, onSubmitted, etc.).

entries.title: Booleans are descriptive
entries.description: Boolean parameters use descriptive adjective/noun names (enabled, dismissible, searchable, loading) rather than is/has-prefixed names.

No dedicated naming-convention tests exist — no test file validates boolean naming discipline
Coverage tracking JSON files for REQ-0015, REQ-0016, REQ-0017 are absent from the EPIC-0003 commit (only REQ-0012, REQ-0013, REQ-0014 have tracking files)

testSummary: No test commands configured. No dedicated naming convention tests exist. Coverage tracking JSON files for REQ-0015, REQ-0016, REQ-0017 are missing from the EPIC-0003 commit. Widget-level behavior tests exist (e.g., test/src/components/buttons/oi_button_test.dart) but do not specifically validate naming convention compliance.
